content.full_text:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").l Since you are not requesting advice on the duties of a
specific public official, we are trqating your request as one for informal assistance
pursuant to Regulation 18329(c).2
Fern Por-rrrcAt- PRACTTcES CoMMrssroN
P.O. Box 807 o 428 J Street . Sacramenro, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 r Fax (916) 322-0886
December 6,2001
Kelly Palmer
City ofRiverside
Office of the City Clerk
3900 Main Street
Riverside, CA92522
Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-01-272
Dear Ms. Palmer
QUESTTON
The city of Riverside is having a run-offelection on January 15,2002. The
closing date for the second pre-election statement is December 29,2OOl,which leaves
only two days for the semi-annual statement for the period ending December 31, 2001.
May committees, which are controlled by or primarily formed to support or oppose
candidates in the January 15,2002, run-off election, combine the semi-annual statement
for the second half of 2001 with the semi-annual statement for the first half of 2002?
1 Government Code Section81000-91014. Commissionregulations appear attitle2,sectionsl8l0g-
18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with ttre immunity provided by an opinion or formal
writtenadvice. (Government Code Section83l14;2 Cal. Code of Regs. Section 18329(c)(3).)
ry


Our File No. I-01-272
Page2
CONCLUSION
Yes. Any committee controlled by or primarily formed to support or oppose a
candidate involved in the January 15,2002, run-off election may combine the semi-
annual statement for the second half of 2001 with the semi-annual statement for the first
half of 2002.
ANALYSIS
Committees controlled by and primarily formed to support or oppose candidates
are required to file two pre-election campaign statements disclosing contributions
received and expenditures made in connection with an election. These pre-election
statements are generally filed 40 days before the election and again l2 days before the
election. (Section 34200.8.) In addition, committees which are controlled or primarily
formed to support or oppose candidates are required to file semi-annual statements on
January 3l and July 31. (Section 84200.)
The City of Riverside is having a run-off election on January 15,2002, following
the Novemb er 6, 2001, election. The first pre-election statement filed in connection with
the run-offelection is due December 6,2001, and covers the reporting period October 2l
through December 1,2001. The second pre-election statement is due January 3,2002,
and covers the reporting period December 2 through December 29,2001.
There is a mechanism in the Act which permits the combining of statements.
(Section 84205.) We are authorizing the combining of the semi-annual statement for the
second half of 200 I with the semi-annual statement for the first half of 2002.
Accordingly, any committees controlled by or primarily formed to support or
oppose a candidate involved in the January 15,2002, run-offelection may combine the
semi-annual statement for the second half of 2001 with the semi-annual statement for the
first half of 2002. The due date forthis statementwill be July 31,2002, covering the
period December 30,2001, through June 30, 2002. A campaign filing schedule that
outlines the due dates for filing statements is enclosed for your convenience.
In addition, committees involved in the election will cumulate contributions
received and expenditures made from January I,2001, through June 30, 2002. Section
82018 provides that "'cumulative amount' means the amount of contributions received or
expenditures made in the calendar year." However, subdivision (b) of section 82018
provides: "[flor a filer required to file a campaign statement or independent expenditure
report in one year in connection with an election to be held in another year, the period
over which the cumulative amount is calculated shall end on the closing date of the first
semiannual statement filed after the election."


Our File No. I-01-272
Page 3
If I may be of any further assistance, please do not hesitate to contact me at (91 6)
322-3660
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General
rl
:l 
I
By: W Imberi
Political Reform Consultant
